calculated_metrics.name: Impact Equivalency Score (IES)

details.criteria: Pass: <= 2 years of expenditure. | Warn: 2-5 years. | Fail: > 5 years.
Pass: >= 6 months of operating expenses. | Warn: 3-6 months. | Fail: < 3 months.
Pass: At least one intervention is classified as Tier 1 (Systemic Change) or Tier 2 (Prevention & Scalable Care). | Warn: All verifiable interventions are Tier 3 (Direct Care & Indirect Action).
details.elaboration: [{"tier_name": "Tier 2: Preventative Scale", "interventions": [{"name": "High Volume Spay Neuter", "source": {"source_type": "attached_report", "source_index": 0, "page_number": 2, "quote": "During the year, a total of 155 dogs and cats were neutered and vaccinated through our Neutering Fund.", "resolved_url": "https://spca-penang.net/annual_reports/annual_report_2024.pdf#page=2"}}]}, {"tier_name": "Tier 3: Direct Care & Indirect Action", "interventions": [{"name": "Individual Rescue And Sanctuary", "source": {"source_type": "attached_report", "source_index": 0, "page_number": 1, "quote": "ADOPTION rate @ the Shelter is low in 2024, with Dogs & puppies (36) and Cats/Kittens (29)", "resolved_url": "https://spca-penang.net/annual_reports/annual_report_2024.pdf#page=1"}}, {"name": "Individual Rescue And Sanctuary", "source": {"source_type": "attached_report", "source_index": 0, "page_number": 1, "quote": "ADOPTION rate @ the Shelter is low in 2024, with Dogs & puppies (36) and Cats/Kittens (29)", "resolved_url": "https://spca-penang.net/annual_reports/annual_report_2024.pdf#page=1"}}]}]

details.calculation: A quantified counterfactual baseline was provided.
details.criteria: Pass: At least one metric has a quantified counterfactual baseline. | Warn: A qualitative baseline is provided, but lacks quantified data. | Fail: No valid counterfactual baseline is provided.
details.elaboration: Quote: 'ADOPTION rate @ the Shelter is low in 2024, with Dogs & puppies (36) and Cats/Kittens (29)'
details.formula: Presence of a quantified counterfactual baseline with a source quote.

details.calculation: Financials with income breakdown are missing.
details.criteria: Pass: Government grants are < 80% of total income. | Fail: Government grants are >= 80% of total income.

details.calculation: Operates in a low-neglectedness / saturated area (Companion Animals: 100%).
details.criteria: Pass: >= 50% of beneficiaries are high-neglectedness (farmed/wild). | Warn: 1-49% are high-neglectedness. | Fail: 0% are high-neglectedness.
details.formula: Proportional analysis of beneficiary populations (farmed/wild vs. companion)

details.calculation: Organisation self-reported on unintended negative impacts.
details.criteria: Bonus: Organisation self-reported on unintended negative impacts. | Not Disclosed: No disclosure found.

Quote: 'ADOPTION rate @ the Shelter is low in 2024, with Dogs & puppies (36) and Cats/Kittens (29)
The continuing Low Rate with each passing year is partly contributed by:

(# Unfortunately, not everyone is keen to release them back to its environment, which leaves SPCA
no option but to release them in areas where there is "food Source"'

details.calculation: No disclosure of euthanasia or live-release rates found.

metrics.metric_name: Dogs and cats neutered and vaccinated

source.quote: During the year, a total of 155 dogs and cats were neutered and vaccinated through our Neutering Fund.

The year 2024 have been a busy year for the Society, as we try hard to meet the Mission of the
Society ie.to protect and improve on the welfare of animals.
We work this goal by:
Providing Shelter & Food
Giving Medical Care & attention to animals in need
Advocating for better animal welfare laws & enforcement
Educating the public about the importance of respecting animals; AND ensuring they live
healthy, happy & dignified -free from pain & suffering. Or at least given proper treatment,
care & love to minimize their discomfort & suffering.

ADOPTION rate @ the Shelter is low in 2024, with Dogs & puppies (36) and Cats/Kittens (29)
The continuing Low Rate with each passing year is partly contributed by:
A) TIGHT BUDGET - faced by the public due to Inflation and High Cost of Living.
(# Unfortunately, not everyone is keen to release them back to its environment, which leaves SPCA
no option but to release them in areas where there is "food Source"

aliases: Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals
